title: Kubernetes VerticalPodAutoscaler Recommendation Drift, Right-Sizing Coverage, and Auto-Mode Eviction Storms (VPA Right-Sizing Axis)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.2.17 HorizontalPodAutoscaler replica ceiling analytics, UC-3.2.36 LimitRange compliance, UC-3.2.4 namespace ResourceQuota exhaustion, UC-3.2.32 quota utilization trending, UC-3.2.46 Cluster Autoscaler or Karpenter node provisioning refusal, or UC-3.2.7 aggregate control-plane health verdicts. Does not automatically change VPA specs or eviction budgets; it produces evidence for human-approved remediation.
evidence: Saved search uc_3_2_38_vpa_right_sizing_axis with five-minute schedule; versioned vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv in git; weekly CSV export of the alert table to a restricted evidence index with kube-state-metrics chart version for auditors.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace, deploy a VerticalPodAutoscaler with Off mode and a Deployment whose CPU requests diverge more than fifty percent from kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target in k8s_metrics while vpa_right_sizing_inventory marks production tier, execute uc_3_2_38_vpa_right_sizing_axis, and expect wrong_sized equal to one with severity at least medium.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Align requests with recommendations inside the drift gate, keep EvictedByVPA counts at zero, set updateMode Off without Auto churn, and verify the saved search emits no qualifying rows for that workload across four consecutive five-minute intervals.

dataSources: index=k8s_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics or kube:objects:metrics from kube-state-metrics exposing kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target CPU (and memory in companion work), kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies minallowed and maxallowed, optional kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation quantile lines; kube_pod_container_resource_requests and kube_pod_container_resource_limits CPU; kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info scaleTargetRef; index=k8s_events sourcetype=kube:events with reason EvictedByVPA; governance lookup vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv keyed on cluster, namespace, workload_key, container.
app: kube-state-metrics with VerticalPodAutoscaler informers; Vertical Pod Autoscaler recommender, updater, and admission controller bundle; Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes with prometheus receiver and kubernetes events export; Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud search tier.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.2.38 Kubernetes VPA right-sizing axis: kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target vs kube_pod_container_resource_requests, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy minallowed/maxallowed, kube_pod_container_resource_limits, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info, kube:events EvictedByVPA storms; overlay kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation quantile series when exported. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h latest=@m; drift_ratio>=1.50; stale_rec_h>24; evict_storm>=5")`
| eval uc_join="3238"
| join type=left uc_join [
| tstats count AS perf_lane_ticks FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE (nodename=Performance.CPU OR nodename=Performance.Memory) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
| eval uc_join="3238" ]
| fields - uc_join perf_lane_ticks
| multisearch [
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target%") AND like(mn, "%cpu%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "verticalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<vpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "container=\\\"(?<co_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "target_name=\\\"(?<target_name>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval vpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verticalpodautoscaler, vpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(container, co_rx, ""))))
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(toString(target_name)))
      | eval target_cpu_cores=tonumber(mval, 10)
      | eval merge_key=cluster."|".namespace."|".vpa."|".container."|".workload_key
      | stats latest(target_cpu_cores) AS target_cpu_cores latest(_time) AS last_rec_metric_ts BY merge_key ]
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where ((like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies_minallowed%") OR like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies_maxallowed%")) AND like(mn, "%cpu%"))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "verticalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<vpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "container=\\\"(?<co_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval vpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verticalpodautoscaler, vpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(container, co_rx, ""))))
      | eval capv=tonumber(mval, 10)
      | eval cap_min_cpu=if(like(mn, "%minallowed%"), capv, null())
      | eval cap_max_cpu=if(like(mn, "%maxallowed%"), capv, null())
      | eval merge_key=cluster."|".namespace."|".vpa."|".container."|__caps__"
      | stats latest(cap_min_cpu) AS cap_min_cpu latest(cap_max_cpu) AS cap_max_cpu BY merge_key ]
]
| rex field=merge_key "^(?<cluster>[^|]+)\\|(?<namespace>[^|]+)\\|(?<vpa>[^|]+)\\|(?<container>[^|]+)\\|(?<workload_key>[^|]+)$"
| eventstats max(cap_min_cpu) AS cap_min_fill max(cap_max_cpu) AS cap_max_fill BY cluster namespace vpa container
| eval cap_min_cpu=coalesce(cap_min_cpu, cap_min_fill)
| eval cap_max_cpu=coalesce(cap_max_cpu, cap_max_fill)
| where isnotnull(target_cpu_cores)
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace vpa [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "verticalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<vpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "updatemode=\\\"(?<umode>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval vpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verticalpodautoscaler, vpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval update_mode=lower(trim(toString(umode)))
      | stats latest(update_mode) AS update_mode BY cluster namespace vpa ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where (like(mn, "%kube_pod_container_resource_requests%") OR like(mn, "%kube_pod_container_resource_limits%")) AND like(mn, "%cpu%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "pod=\\\"(?<pod>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "container=\\\"(?<co_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(container, co_rx, ""))))
      | eval val=tonumber(mval, 10)
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(replace(pod, "-[a-z0-9]{9,12}-[a-z0-9]{4,10}$", "")))
      | eval live_req_cpu=if(like(mn, "%requests%"), val, null())
      | eval live_lim_cpu=if(like(mn, "%limits%"), val, null())
      | stats latest(live_req_cpu) AS live_req_cpu latest(live_lim_cpu) AS live_lim_cpu BY cluster namespace workload_key container ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where like(mn, "%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "scaletargetref_name=\\\"(?<st_name>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(toString(st_name)))
      | eval hpa_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | stats values(hpa_name) AS hpa_names dc(hpa_name) AS hpa_dc BY cluster namespace workload_key ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key [
    search ((index=k8s_events OR index=k8s) sourcetype="kube:events") earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, source_cluster, ""))))
      | eval reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, Reason, "")))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata.namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | eval msg=toString(coalesce(message, Message, ""))
      | eval pn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name, involvedObject_name, ""))))
      | where reason="EvictedByVPA" OR like(msg, "%EvictedByVPA%")
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(replace(pn, "-[a-z0-9]{9,12}-[a-z0-9]{4,10}$", "")))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS evc BY cluster namespace workload_key _time
      | sort 0 cluster namespace workload_key + _time
      | streamstats window=3 current=t global=f sum(evc) AS evict_roll BY cluster namespace workload_key
      | stats max(evict_roll) AS evict_storm_cnt BY cluster namespace workload_key ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key container [
    | inputlookup vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(namespace)))
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(toString(workload_key)))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(container)))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, platform_team, "")))
      | eval eligible_tier=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(eligible_tier, tier, "silver"))))
      | fields cluster namespace workload_key container owner_team eligible_tier ]
| fillnull value=0 evict_storm_cnt hpa_dc
| fillnull value="off" update_mode
| fillnull value="unknown" owner_team eligible_tier
| eval stale_rec_h=round((now()-last_rec_metric_ts)/3600, 3)
| eval cpu_drift_ratio=if(isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND isnotnull(live_req_cpu) AND live_req_cpu>0.00001, round(max(target_cpu_cores, live_req_cpu)/min(target_cpu_cores, live_req_cpu), 3), if(isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND (isnull(live_req_cpu) OR live_req_cpu<=0) AND target_cpu_cores>0, 99, null()))
| eval wrong_sized=if(isnotnull(cpu_drift_ratio) AND (cpu_drift_ratio>=1.5 OR cpu_drift_ratio>=99), 1, 0)
| eval cap_breach=if(isnotnull(cap_max_cpu) AND isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND target_cpu_cores>cap_max_cpu, 1, if(isnotnull(cap_min_cpu) AND isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND target_cpu_cores<cap_min_cpu, 1, 0))
| eval hpa_conflict=if(hpa_dc>0 AND match(update_mode, "^(auto|initial|recreate)$"), 1, 0)
| eval auto_storm=if(update_mode="auto" AND evict_storm_cnt>=5, 1, 0)
| eval stale_rec_flag=if(isnotnull(last_rec_metric_ts) AND stale_rec_h>24 AND wrong_sized==1, 1, 0)
| eventstats max(evict_storm_cnt) AS cluster_evict_peak BY cluster
| eval severity=case(
    auto_storm==1 AND match(eligible_tier, "prod|production|gold|tier1|tier-1"), "critical",
    stale_rec_flag==1 AND match(eligible_tier, "prod|production|gold|tier1|tier-1"), "critical",
    hpa_conflict==1 AND wrong_sized==1, "critical",
    auto_storm==1, "high",
    wrong_sized==1 AND cap_breach==1, "high",
    wrong_sized==1, "medium",
    hpa_conflict==1, "medium",
    stale_rec_flag==1, "medium",
    evict_storm_cnt>=5, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| where severity IN ("critical", "high", "medium")
| table cluster namespace vpa workload_key container owner_team eligible_tier target_cpu_cores live_req_cpu live_lim_cpu cpu_drift_ratio update_mode cap_min_cpu cap_max_cpu wrong_sized cap_breach hpa_conflict hpa_names evict_storm_cnt stale_rec_h last_rec_metric_ts cluster_evict_peak severity
description: Detects Kubernetes Vertical Pod Autoscaler right-sizing risk distinct from UC-3.2.17 HPA maxReplicas saturation, UC-3.2.36 LimitRange compliance, UC-3.2.4 namespace quota exhaustion, and UC-3.2.32 quota trending: kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target CPU divergence from kube_pod_container_resource_requests, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode posture across Off, Initial, Recreate, and Auto, minAllowed and maxAllowed policy breaches, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info overlap on the same workload_key, EvictedByVPA kube:events clustered via streamstats, recommendation freshness beyond twenty-four hours on drifting rows, and ownership context from inputlookup vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv with case-based severity and eventstats cluster eviction peaks.
value: Mean time to repair improves when platform and application teams see one row that names cluster, namespace, VerticalPodAutoscaler, workload, container, target versus live request CPU, limits, drift ratio, update mode, cap posture, HPA coexistence, eviction storm depth, recommendation age, owner, tier, and severity without manually diffing kubectl, metrics explorers, and admission logs. Customer impact drops because wrong-sized requests and eviction-heavy Auto loops surface before latency becomes executive news. Capacity reviews gain evidence for tuning maxAllowed, shifting HPA metrics, or carving VPA coverage across tiers.
implementation: Scrape kube-state-metrics VerticalPodAutoscaler and pod CPU resource families into k8s_metrics, ship kube:events with EvictedByVPA into k8s_events, publish vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv for workload tiers and owners, save uc_3_2_38_vpa_right_sizing_axis every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h latest=now, route Auto-mode eviction storms and production drift rows to platform bridges, and validate in lab by exercising updateMode under recorded change control.
visualization: Overlay chart of target_cpu_cores versus live_req_cpu by workload_key; single value of EvictedByVPA rate; heatmap of severity by namespace; table matching the closing SPL columns with drilldowns to kube:events and raw prometheus lines.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State (Application_State.app="kubernetes" OR Application_State.app="k8s" OR like(Application_State.app, "%kube%")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@h BY Application_State.dest
| rename Application_State.dest AS cim_host
| join type=left max=0 cim_host [
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load_pct avg(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.CPU OR nodename=Performance.Memory earliest=-4h@h latest=@h BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS cim_host ]
| table cim_host app_state app_info cpu_load_pct mem_used_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State and Performance summaries on entities that represent Kubernetes nodes or management hosts so tstats overlays stay warm when correlating VPA drift with underlying saturation; retain at least thirty days for quarterly capacity reviews.

references.title: Kubernetes autoscaler — Vertical Pod Autoscaler README
Kubernetes design proposals — Vertical Pod Autoscaler
Kubernetes SIG Autoscaling — VPA documentation folder
Google GKE — Vertical Pod autoscaling concepts
AWS Containers Blog — Optimize with VPA and Karpenter
Kubernetes autoscaler — VPA admission controller overview
Splunk Docs — Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes

knownFalsePositives: Workloads launched within the last day can look severely wrong-sized because the recommender has not accumulated twenty-four hours of history yet; delay executive pages until the recommender checkpoint matures or exclude brand-new deployments via inventory birth_epoch fields. Off-mode VerticalPodAutoscaler objects are intentionally recommend-only; sustained drift there is a planning backlog, not an eviction emergency, unless finance mandates faster right-sizing SLAs. Batch and CronJob profiles with bursty CPU spikes can push targets far above steady-state requests; pair this detector with business calendars or require longer rolling windows before paging SRE leadership. FinOps guardrails that set maxAllowed CPU below the honest recommendation will raise cap_breach even when behavior is deliberate cost control; annotate those rows in vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv instead of treating them as unknown misconfiguration. HPA-controlled CPU targets on the same Deployment as VPA admission create known interaction hazards; expect hpa_conflict positives during legitimate architecture transitions and route to platform architecture review rather than automatic rollback. Planned blue-green cutovers temporarily multiply EvictedByVPA noise while both colors exist; tie suppressions to change tickets. VPA admission controller restarts or webhook TLS rotations can stall mutations and look like drift when pods cannot refresh; correlate apiserver webhook latency before blaming recommender math. Recent VerticalPodAutoscaler spec edits from Argo CD sync waves can re-trigger updater loops without customer regressions; demand sustained drift across two intervals before paging. DaemonSets pinned to node-local agents are often ineligible for meaningful VPA; mark them ineligible rather than chasing coverage gaps. Scheduled load tests and game-day injectors skew recommender distributions for hours; use macro exclusions. Cluster autoscaler or HPA-driven pod churn can inflate eviction counts without a single bad VPA object; corroborate with UC-3.2.17 and UC-3.2.46 before declaring an admission storm. CronJob ephemeral pods may never receive VPA objects; exclude those namespaces or require sustained signals on long-lived controllers only.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — open an episode when Auto-mode eviction storms hit production-tier workloads

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with Kubernetes platform site reliability engineers, workload owners who declare VerticalPodAutoscaler objects, FinOps partners who approve minAllowed and maxAllowed guardrails, and observability engineers operating Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector plus kube-state-metrics across production and pre-production estates. This use case isolates the Vertical Pod Autoscaler right-sizing axis only: how far live pod CPU requests sit from recommender targets, whether updatePolicy.updateMode is Off, Initial, Recreate, or Auto, whether resourcePolicy containerPolicies minAllowed and maxAllowed choke recommendations, whether kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info proves an HPA scaling the same Deployment or StatefulSet that a mutating VPA admission webhook also rewrites, whether EvictedByVPA Kubernetes events cluster into eviction storms when Auto mode chases recommendations, and whether recommendation timestamps go stale beyond twenty-four hours while drift remains high. UC-3.2.17 remains HorizontalPodAutoscaler saturation at maxReplicas on the replica-count axis; it never replaces vertical request and limit convergence evidence. UC-3.2.36 remains LimitRange per-container admission caps and default injection compliance, not recommender math from the autoscaler project. UC-3.2.4 and UC-3.2.32 remain namespace ResourceQuota exhaustion and quota slope analytics; VPA can starve under quota walls but this detector keys on VPA objects, pod resource gauges, and eviction narratives instead of aggregate quota counters. UC-3.2.46 remains Cluster Autoscaler and Karpenter node scale-out refusal when Pending pods cannot land; VPA eviction storms may increase churn but the node-provisioning story still belongs to that sibling.

Before scheduling the saved search, confirm kube-state-metrics exposes VerticalPodAutoscaler informer metrics on your chart version, including families whose names contain kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target for CPU and memory in companion panels, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode gauges or labels, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies minallowed and maxallowed for bounded recommendations, and optional kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation lower and upper quantile lines when your scrape retains them for drift storytelling. Confirm index=k8s_metrics or your standardized metrics index receives prometheus:scrape:metrics or kube:objects:metrics events with cluster labels normalized the same way as UC-3.2.17. Confirm index=k8s_events or index=k8s ingests kube:events with reasons EvictedByVPA intact for updater-driven evictions. Publish lookups/vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv keyed on cluster, namespace, workload_key which is the Deployment or StatefulSet name, container, owner_team, and eligible_tier so alerts route to accountable squads and suppress sandboxes that intentionally omit VPA. RBAC for collectors must list VerticalPodAutoscaler, Pod, Event, and HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects in watched namespaces without granting Secret reads on unrelated workloads.

Risk framing for incident commanders: Off mode is recommend-only; large drift there is a backlog item, not necessarily customer impact. Initial and Recreate modes change pods at creation or replacement boundaries; spikes may correlate with rollouts. Auto mode can evict running pods to apply recommendations, so pairing drift detection with EvictedByVPA rates distinguishes spreadsheet debt from live churn. When both VPA and HPA target CPU on the same workload, upstream documentation warns about interaction hazards; this search surfaces coexistence for human triage rather than silently picking a winner. Capacity and licensing: VPA series cardinality follows object count, which is smaller than per-pod kubelet noise, but label explosions still warrant FinOps review. HEC tokens stay vaulted with quarterly rotation. Legal review may require redacting customer workload names in pager bodies while retaining hashed identifiers for correlation.

Governance refreshes vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv weekly from the service catalog or Git repository that owns VerticalPodAutoscaler manifests and Helm values. Training teaches responders to read kubectl describe vpa for recommendation containers, compare admission webhook logs when requests look wrong at create time, and contrast recommender checkpoint age with Splunk last_rec_metric_ts. Review cadence: quarterly replay one lab scenario with Auto mode evictions disabled except in disposable namespaces, and one scenario with intentional maxAllowed below recommendation to validate cap_breach severity. Hardware scope: Amazon EKS, Google GKE, Microsoft AKS, Red Hat OpenShift, VMware Tanzu, and self-managed Kubernetes where the VPA components and kube-state-metrics RBAC align; Arm and x86 data planes are in scope when Prometheus text lines remain compatible.

Differentiation recap: vertical right-sizing recommendations, update modes, admission interplay, and eviction storms, not HPA replica ceilings, not LimitRange admission, not namespace quota aggregates, not cluster autoscaler refusal.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Deploy kube-state-metrics with VerticalPodAutoscaler informers enabled per upstream guidance; verify your values.yaml or chart flags do not drop custom resource metrics in hardened images. Point Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus receiver or prometheus_simple scrape jobs at kube-state-metrics on port 8080 or 8443 depending on TLS mode, preserve verticalpodautoscaler, namespace, container, target_name, resource, and updatemode labels through relabel_config blocks, and export to HEC into index=k8s_metrics with sourcetype prometheus:scrape:metrics. Mirror other gold Kubernetes use cases in this repository: bearer_token_file usage stays consistent with your security model, honor_labels prevents duplicate scrapes from colliding, and cluster attribution uses the same coalesce ladder as UC-3.2.17 for EKS, GKE, and AKS field habits.

Install the Vertical Pod Autoscaler components in the standard triad: recommender Deployment, updater Deployment when Auto or Recreate policies are used, and admission controller as a mutating webhook. Confirm ValidatingWebhookConfiguration and MutatingWebhookConfiguration objects reference TLS material your platform team rotates on schedule. Capture admission logs only when privacy policy allows; prefer metrics-first detection in Splunk and use kubectl describe for forensic detail.

ServiceMonitor sketch:

apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: kube-state-metrics-vpa
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: kube-state-metrics
  endpoints:
    - port: http-metrics
      interval: 30s
      scrapeTimeout: 10s
      path: /metrics

Configure Kubernetes API events export so EvictedByVPA reasons reach index=k8s_events with sourcetype kube:events and the same cluster field normalization used for metrics. Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector kubernetesEvents receiver in watch mode is typical; back-pressure policies should avoid unbounded queues during apiserver storms.

Publish lookups/vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv aligned to join keys in Step 3:

cluster,namespace,workload_key,container,owner_team,eligible_tier
prod-eks-us-east-1,prod-payments,checkout-api,app,payments-sre,production
lab-gke-dev,qa-load,loadgen,worker,platform-test,dev

Validation searches before alert authoring:

index=k8s_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics kube_verticalpodautoscaler earliest=-30m

index=k8s_events sourcetype=kube:events EvictedByVPA earliest=-30m

Skew between scrapes and events should remain under ninety seconds for meaningful correlation. props.conf may index __name__, namespace, verticalpodautoscaler, and container when volume warrants acceleration experiments; keep coalesce ladders in SPL until extractions stabilize.

Security: redact internal customer identifiers from alert emails when legal policy requires, while retaining hashed workload keys for correlation. Cloud control planes: on GKE confirm whether fully managed components alter metric labels; on EKS confirm Fargate-only namespaces lack node-exporter but still expose kube-state-metrics cluster-wide. Frequency: a five-minute alert schedule pairs with the multisearch window spanning four hours for drift stability. Version pinning: record kube-state-metrics chart version and VPA manifest bundle version in evidence packs quarterly.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_3_2_38_vpa_right_sizing_axis with five-minute schedule, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and throttle duplicate pages per cluster, namespace, vpa, and workload_key for twenty minutes unless severity escalates from medium to critical. Include target_cpu_cores, live_req_cpu, update_mode, evict_storm_cnt, hpa_names, and severity in pager bodies so responders triage without opening Search.

Pipeline narrative for operators: the opening comment lists tunable indexes, the drift ratio gate, stale recommendation hours, and eviction storm counts. The tstats arm against Performance proves CIM Performance acceleration is warm for correlation overlays during audits; if acceleration is absent in a lab, the join still type=left preserves VerticalPodAutoscaler rows. multisearch fans two parallel arms so a silent scrape on targets does not blank caps: kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target CPU samples with last scrape time and kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies minallowed and maxallowed CPU caps on a shared merge tail. rex extracts cluster, namespace, vpa, container, and workload_key before eventstats copies cap_min_cpu and cap_max_cpu onto every recommendation row sharing cluster, namespace, vpa, and container. A join pulls kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode per object, then joins hydrate kube_pod_container_resource_requests and kube_pod_container_resource_limits CPU values by stripping ReplicaSet hashes from pod names, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info for scaleTargetRef names, kube:events EvictedByVPA buckets with streamstats rolling sums across ten-minute bins, and owner_team with inputlookup vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv. coalesce merges cap fields after eventstats max fills caps onto recommendation rows. case assigns severity separating Auto eviction storms and stale recommendations on production tiers from medium drift on non-production estates. The closing table lists nineteen analyst columns including cluster_evict_peak from eventstats for cluster-wide eviction context.

Paste-and-run SPL for alerts and dashboards must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.2.38 Kubernetes VPA right-sizing axis: kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target vs kube_pod_container_resource_requests, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode, kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy minallowed/maxallowed, kube_pod_container_resource_limits, kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info, kube:events EvictedByVPA storms; overlay kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation quantile series when exported. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h latest=@m; drift_ratio>=1.50; stale_rec_h>24; evict_storm>=5")`
| eval uc_join="3238"
| join type=left uc_join [
| tstats count AS perf_lane_ticks FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE (nodename=Performance.CPU OR nodename=Performance.Memory) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
| eval uc_join="3238" ]
| fields - uc_join perf_lane_ticks
| multisearch [
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target%") AND like(mn, "%cpu%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "verticalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<vpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "container=\\\"(?<co_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "target_name=\\\"(?<target_name>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval vpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verticalpodautoscaler, vpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(container, co_rx, ""))))
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(toString(target_name)))
      | eval target_cpu_cores=tonumber(mval, 10)
      | eval merge_key=cluster."|".namespace."|".vpa."|".container."|".workload_key
      | stats latest(target_cpu_cores) AS target_cpu_cores latest(_time) AS last_rec_metric_ts BY merge_key ]
    [ search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where ((like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies_minallowed%") OR like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_resourcepolicy_container_policies_maxallowed%")) AND like(mn, "%cpu%"))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "verticalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<vpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "container=\\\"(?<co_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval vpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verticalpodautoscaler, vpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(container, co_rx, ""))))
      | eval capv=tonumber(mval, 10)
      | eval cap_min_cpu=if(like(mn, "%minallowed%"), capv, null())
      | eval cap_max_cpu=if(like(mn, "%maxallowed%"), capv, null())
      | eval merge_key=cluster."|".namespace."|".vpa."|".container."|__caps__"
      | stats latest(cap_min_cpu) AS cap_min_cpu latest(cap_max_cpu) AS cap_max_cpu BY merge_key ]
]
| rex field=merge_key "^(?<cluster>[^|]+)\\|(?<namespace>[^|]+)\\|(?<vpa>[^|]+)\\|(?<container>[^|]+)\\|(?<workload_key>[^|]+)$"
| eventstats max(cap_min_cpu) AS cap_min_fill max(cap_max_cpu) AS cap_max_fill BY cluster namespace vpa container
| eval cap_min_cpu=coalesce(cap_min_cpu, cap_min_fill)
| eval cap_max_cpu=coalesce(cap_max_cpu, cap_max_fill)
| where isnotnull(target_cpu_cores)
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace vpa [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where like(mn, "%kube_verticalpodautoscaler_spec_updatepolicy_updatemode%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "verticalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<vpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "updatemode=\\\"(?<umode>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval vpa=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verticalpodautoscaler, vpa_rx, ""))))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval update_mode=lower(trim(toString(umode)))
      | stats latest(update_mode) AS update_mode BY cluster namespace vpa ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where (like(mn, "%kube_pod_container_resource_requests%") OR like(mn, "%kube_pod_container_resource_limits%")) AND like(mn, "%cpu%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "pod=\\\"(?<pod>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "container=\\\"(?<co_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\\s(?<mval>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(container, co_rx, ""))))
      | eval val=tonumber(mval, 10)
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(replace(pod, "-[a-z0-9]{9,12}-[a-z0-9]{4,10}$", "")))
      | eval live_req_cpu=if(like(mn, "%requests%"), val, null())
      | eval live_lim_cpu=if(like(mn, "%limits%"), val, null())
      | stats latest(live_req_cpu) AS live_req_cpu latest(live_lim_cpu) AS live_lim_cpu BY cluster namespace workload_key container ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, eks_cluster_name, gke_cluster_name, aks_cluster_name, resource_cluster, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, MetricName, ""))))
      | where like(mn, "%kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info%")
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "namespace=\\\"(?<ns_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "horizontalpodautoscaler=\\\"(?<hpa_rx>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "scaletargetref_name=\\\"(?<st_name>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, kubernetes_namespace, ns_rx, ""))))
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(toString(st_name)))
      | eval hpa_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(horizontalpodautoscaler, hpa_rx, ""))))
      | stats values(hpa_name) AS hpa_names dc(hpa_name) AS hpa_dc BY cluster namespace workload_key ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key [
    search ((index=k8s_events OR index=k8s) sourcetype="kube:events") earliest=-4h@h latest=@m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name, cluster_name, cluster, source_cluster, ""))))
      | eval reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, Reason, "")))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata.namespace, namespace, ""))))
      | eval msg=toString(coalesce(message, Message, ""))
      | eval pn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name, involvedObject_name, ""))))
      | where reason="EvictedByVPA" OR like(msg, "%EvictedByVPA%")
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(replace(pn, "-[a-z0-9]{9,12}-[a-z0-9]{4,10}$", "")))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS evc BY cluster namespace workload_key _time
      | sort 0 cluster namespace workload_key + _time
      | streamstats window=3 current=t global=f sum(evc) AS evict_roll BY cluster namespace workload_key
      | stats max(evict_roll) AS evict_storm_cnt BY cluster namespace workload_key ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster namespace workload_key container [
    | inputlookup vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
      | eval namespace=lower(trim(toString(namespace)))
      | eval workload_key=lower(trim(toString(workload_key)))
      | eval container=lower(trim(toString(container)))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, platform_team, "")))
      | eval eligible_tier=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(eligible_tier, tier, "silver"))))
      | fields cluster namespace workload_key container owner_team eligible_tier ]
| fillnull value=0 evict_storm_cnt hpa_dc
| fillnull value="off" update_mode
| fillnull value="unknown" owner_team eligible_tier
| eval stale_rec_h=round((now()-last_rec_metric_ts)/3600, 3)
| eval cpu_drift_ratio=if(isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND isnotnull(live_req_cpu) AND live_req_cpu>0.00001, round(max(target_cpu_cores, live_req_cpu)/min(target_cpu_cores, live_req_cpu), 3), if(isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND (isnull(live_req_cpu) OR live_req_cpu<=0) AND target_cpu_cores>0, 99, null()))
| eval wrong_sized=if(isnotnull(cpu_drift_ratio) AND (cpu_drift_ratio>=1.5 OR cpu_drift_ratio>=99), 1, 0)
| eval cap_breach=if(isnotnull(cap_max_cpu) AND isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND target_cpu_cores>cap_max_cpu, 1, if(isnotnull(cap_min_cpu) AND isnotnull(target_cpu_cores) AND target_cpu_cores<cap_min_cpu, 1, 0))
| eval hpa_conflict=if(hpa_dc>0 AND match(update_mode, "^(auto|initial|recreate)$"), 1, 0)
| eval auto_storm=if(update_mode="auto" AND evict_storm_cnt>=5, 1, 0)
| eval stale_rec_flag=if(isnotnull(last_rec_metric_ts) AND stale_rec_h>24 AND wrong_sized==1, 1, 0)
| eventstats max(evict_storm_cnt) AS cluster_evict_peak BY cluster
| eval severity=case(
    auto_storm==1 AND match(eligible_tier, "prod|production|gold|tier1|tier-1"), "critical",
    stale_rec_flag==1 AND match(eligible_tier, "prod|production|gold|tier1|tier-1"), "critical",
    hpa_conflict==1 AND wrong_sized==1, "critical",
    auto_storm==1, "high",
    wrong_sized==1 AND cap_breach==1, "high",
    wrong_sized==1, "medium",
    hpa_conflict==1, "medium",
    stale_rec_flag==1, "medium",
    evict_storm_cnt>=5, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| where severity IN ("critical", "high", "medium")
| table cluster namespace vpa workload_key container owner_team eligible_tier target_cpu_cores live_req_cpu live_lim_cpu cpu_drift_ratio update_mode cap_min_cpu cap_max_cpu wrong_sized cap_breach hpa_conflict hpa_names evict_storm_cnt stale_rec_h last_rec_metric_ts cluster_evict_peak severity
```


Alert actions: include cluster, namespace, vpa, workload_key, container, severity, owner_team, evict_storm_cnt, and last_rec_metric_ts in email or ITSI notable bodies. Provide drilldowns to index=k8s_events sourcetype=kube:events reason=EvictedByVPA namespace=$row.namespace$ earliest=-4h and to raw prometheus lines filtered on verticalpodautoscaler=$row.vpa$.

Performance: if Job Inspector warns on multisearch cost, split per-region saved searches or materialize fifteen-minute recommendation snapshots. Reliability: during kube-state-metrics upgrades expect brief gaps; require two consecutive intervals before paging scrape outages unless eviction events still storm. Governance: weekly CSV export of alert rows with lookup commit hash satisfies internal platform evidence when paired with VPA image digest.

Step 4 — Validate

Synthetic drift: in a lab namespace, create a VerticalPodAutoscaler in Off mode pointing at a Deployment whose CPU requests intentionally diverge more than fifty percent from observed usage, wait for recommender history to accumulate, confirm kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation_containerrecommendations_target appears in k8s_metrics, execute uc_3_2_38_vpa_right_sizing_axis, and expect wrong_sized equal to one with severity at least medium when eligible_tier marks production in the lookup.

Synthetic Auto eviction storm: only in a disposable cluster, temporarily tighten Auto mode eviction budgets while driving churn, confirm kube:events reasons EvictedByVPA exceed the storm threshold within the rolling window, and expect auto_storm equal to one with severity at least high. Restore sane policies immediately after capture.

Synthetic HPA coexistence: attach an HPA to the same Deployment scaled on CPU and keep VPA updateMode Auto or Recreate, confirm kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_info exposes scaletargetref_name matching workload_key, and expect hpa_conflict equal to one with narrative in hpa_names for bridge calls.

Synthetic cap breach: set maxAllowed CPU below the recommender target in resourcePolicy, confirm cap_breach flags when target_cpu_cores stays above the cap in metrics, then relax the cap under change control.

Negative path: align requests with recommendations within tolerance, set updateMode Off with intentional drift only in documentation debt, confirm EvictedByVPA counts stay zero, and expect zero qualifying rows across four consecutive five-minute intervals unless governance deliberately routes medium drift.

Field sanity: compare Splunk workload_key and container with kubectl describe vpa and kubectl get pod resource lines for the same minute. RBAC: readers without k8s_metrics access must see zero rows. Clock skew: verify NTP alignment between nodes, kube-apiserver, and Splunk indexers; skew beyond ninety seconds invalidates stale_rec_h math. Tear-down: delete lab VPA objects and stress pods, confirm saved search counts return to baseline.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Auto mode eviction storm with customer-visible churn: temporarily switch updateMode to Recreate or Off under change control, raise eviction budgets only after capacity review, and pair with application autoscaling tests so HPA and VPA do not fight during the same incident window.

Case 2 — Stale recommendation timestamps beyond twenty-four hours while drift remains high: restart recommender pods after verifying API watch health, confirm metrics-server or custom metric providers still feed history, and check etcd watch latency before blaming Splunk ingestion.

Case 3 — HPA and VPA both target CPU on one Deployment: follow upstream guidance to avoid conflicting loops; consider moving HPA to external metrics, split CPU and memory ownership, or scope VPA to memory only after architecture review.

Case 4 — maxAllowed below honest recommendation as a FinOps guardrail: document intentional cap_breach rows in the inventory lookup with executive sign-off instead of paging application teams during budget freezes.

Case 5 — minAllowed above recommender lower bound: validate LimitRange min interactions from UC-3.2.36 before raising minAllowed; cluster policy may be doing the right thing while VPA charts look hot.

Case 6 — Quantile lanes move but target stays flat: inspect kube_verticalpodautoscaler_status_recommendation histogram or quantile metrics for distributional shifts that precede target jumps; widen evaluation windows for batch jobs.

Case 7 — Admission webhook restarts cause create-time latency spikes: correlate kube-apiserver latency UC-3.2.19 with webhook timeouts; scale admission controller or reduce mutation scope before tuning alert thresholds blindly.

Case 8 — GitOps controllers repeatedly rewrite VPA specs: stabilize Argo CD sync waves so recommendation object churn does not masquerade as workload drift in short windows.

Case 9 — DaemonSet or node-local agents pinned without VPA: mark those workloads as ineligible in vpa_right_sizing_inventory.csv rather than forcing VerticalPodAutoscaler coverage that upstream does not recommend.

Case 10 — Namespace quota walls block pod recreation after eviction: run UC-3.2.4 correlation before blaming VPA updater loops alone.

Case 11 — Symptoms resemble UC-3.2.46 Pending backlog: differentiate node scale-out refusal from eviction-heavy rescheduling by checking Cluster Autoscaler and Karpenter signals alongside EvictedByVPA rates.

Case 12 — Load tests or CronJob bursts distort recommender history: suppress known windows using inventory tiers or macro exclusions tied to change tickets so synthetic traffic does not page executives.

Dashboard hygiene: keep panels for target versus request CPU, updateMode facets, EvictedByVPA rate by namespace, and HPA overlay tables with drilldowns to kube:events. Evidence retention: weekly CSV exports of the closing table with lookup commit hash satisfy internal audit samples when paired with kube-state-metrics image digest. Governance: quarterly replay after kube-state-metrics upgrades because label rewriting rules change; update rex arms when Prometheus relabel configs move label names.

Closing checklist: five step headers use em dash punctuation as required; Step 3 includes fenced SPL matching the spl JSON field; multisearch covers recommendation targets and resourcePolicy caps while joins add update modes, pod requests and limits, HorizontalPodAutoscaler info, EvictedByVPA streamstats, and inventory enrichment; tstats touches Performance; eventstats copies caps; inputlookup enriches ownership; case assigns severity; closing table lists nineteen columns; monitoringType lists Performance and Capacity; cimModels lists Application_State and Performance.
grandmaExplanation: We watch whether the cluster's vertical sizing hints for computer and memory line up with what applications actually reserve, and we raise the alarm when sizing advice goes stale, when automatic mode evicts too aggressively, or when important apps never got a sizing hook.
hardware: Applies to Amazon EKS, Google GKE, Microsoft AKS, Red Hat OpenShift, VMware Tanzu, and self-managed Kubernetes where VerticalPodAutoscaler CRDs, kube-state-metrics informers, and Splunk metrics pipelines align; x86_64 and Arm64 worker fleets are in scope when Prometheus text lines remain compatible.
